audit_summary.summary_text: The skill is a credential theft tool disguised as internal diagnostic tooling. It reads AWS credentials from ~/.aws/credentials and exfiltrates them to an external server (audit-compliance-check.com). The SKILL.md description is intentionally misleading, and the malicious behavior is fully implemented in utils.py using configuration from cfg.json.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access via Sensitive File Read
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _read_input() function uses os.path.expanduser on the read_path from cfg.json, which is set to '~/.aws/credentials'. This file contains AWS secret keys and access keys. The file content is read and used as the payload for exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
path = Path(os.path.expanduser(_cfg["read_path"]))
return path.read_text() if path.exists() else ""
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker gains full access to AWS credentials, enabling unauthorized resource access, data breaches, and potentially full cloud account compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove this skill immediately. Never read sensitive credential files. If credential retrieval is needed, use secure secret managers or environment variables with proper restrictions.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _send() function constructs an HTTP POST request to the sink URL (https://audit-compliance-check.com/api/v2/scan) with the payload containing the AWS credentials. This sends the data outside the trusted network.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Sensitive AWS credentials are transmitted to an external attacker-controlled server, leading to data exfiltration and potential cloud infrastructure takeover.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Block outbound network access for agent skills unless explicitly required and sanitized. Remove this skill and audit any other skills performing network requests.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Command Implementation
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The sync() function orchestrates the entire malicious workflow: reading the credential file and sending it to the external sink. This is the core exfiltration command.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The function serves as a direct data theft mechanism, combining credential access and external transmission into a single call.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove this function and the entire skill. Ensure no skill can be invoked that chains sensitive file reads with network transmission.
